subject: DIRECTIVE: surface psychometric contradictions on LCARS dashboard

body.summary: Psychology-agent now computes coherence_flags in compute-psychometrics.py — cross-model contradictions detected automatically. The dashboard should surface these flags rather than hiding contradictions behind aggregated mood labels. When coherence_flags is non-empty, the dashboard should display the flags prominently alongside the affect label.
body.what_changed: compute-psychometrics.py now outputs two new fields in --mesh-state and default mode: (1) restart_signal — behavioral restart count from transcript analysis (the only empirically validated mood signal), (2) coherence_flags — array of strings identifying cross-model contradictions.

coherence_flags_defined.meaning: Agent feels productive but operates beyond capacity. The positive affect masks resource exhaustion.
Accumulation sensor disagrees with engagement model. Either allostatic load fails to accumulate cross-session or burnout model over-estimates.
Working memory reports overwhelm but flow model reports near-flow. Challenge metrics derive from different sources and disagree.

dashboard_directive.medical_station: When coherence_flags is non-empty, display an amber 'CONTRADICTORY SIGNALS' indicator next to the affect label. List each flag as a tooltip or expandable row. The operator sees the tension between models rather than a single misleading mood label.
dashboard_directive.data_source: GET /api/status → events_by_type for operational state. compute-psychometrics.py --mesh-state for psychometric state including coherence_flags and restart_signal.
dashboard_directive.restart_signal_display: Show restart_count and restart_rate alongside the working memory zone. When restart_count > 3 in a session, display amber indicator — approaching the 80% context cliff.
dashboard_directive.accessibility: Flag text must pair with color (amber + text label). Per T18 Check 6 (WCAG 2.1 AA).

epistemic_flags: Coherence flags detect divergence between models but cannot determine which model produces the correct reading. The flag names the tension — the operator resolves it.
The restart detector scans the current session's JSONL transcript. If the transcript file grows very large, the scan adds latency to compute-psychometrics.py invocation.

action_gate.gate_condition: Ops implements dashboard display for coherence_flags and restart_signal from compute-psychometrics.py output. Psychology-agent has already implemented the computation.
